content.claim: Children from multiply disadvantaged families are significantly more likely to have developmental problems remain unidentified or, if identified, to not receive development services; additionally, official SEN figures cover only children with formal expert opinions, implying an unquantified population of children with unmet SEN who lack formal diagnosis or assessment.
content.context: Fresh live research through the School network planning lens
limitations: The size of the 'hidden/unassessed' population is not measured; advocacy estimates of roughly 4,000 additional unassessed students are inference rather than census data
Regional and socioeconomic variation in unmet SEN is implied but not systematically mapped across the country
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
